title: Fix display with no % printing assoc array
body:
```
Vtop___024root__DepSet_h84412442__3.cpp:35512:45: error: cannot pass object of non-trivial type 'VlAssocArray<IData, VlWide<8>>' (aka 'VlAssocArray<unsigned int, VlWide<8>>') through variadic function; call will abort at runtime [-Wnon-pod-varargs]
                  vlSymsp->name(),0,vlSelf->top__DOT__testbench__DOT__tl2_mem__DOT__mem);
                                            ^
```
C++ code:
```
        VL_WRITEF("[%0t] Loading %s into %Ntop.load_program\n%1#\n",
                  64,VL_TIME_UNITED_Q(1000),-9,2048,
                  vlSelf->top__DOT__load_program__DOT__testfile.data(),
                  vlSymsp->name(),0,vlSelf->top__DOT__testbench__DOT__tl2_mem__DOT__mem);
```
Verilog:
```
       $display("[%0t] Loading %s into %m\n", $time, testfile, `TESTBENCH_RAM_PATH);
```
Thanks for taking the time to report this.

Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?
```
v5.012-52-g23fe5c1b9
```
What OS and distribution are you using?
```
Linux loran 4.18.0-500.el8.x86_64 #1 SMP Wed Jun 28 00:07:07 UTC 2023 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```
May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

Maybe
